general/001.mp4: This realistic-style video documents a quiet park chess scene, set beside a stone table in a city park in the early morning, with neatly trimmed shrubs, a damp stone-paved path, and blurred benches in the distance as the background. There are two people in the video: on the left, a man wearing a dark green jacket, and on the right, a man wearing a gray knit cap and a black coat. Both sit on opposite sides of the chessboard, their expressions focused, with chess pieces neatly arranged on the center of the table. The video begins with the camera starting steadily from an equal center position between the two, both framed from the chest up, with the lower edge of the chessboard faintly visible. The man on the left looks at the board and says in a low tone: <S>If you move the bishop now, you leave your king exposed.<E> The man on the right pauses briefly, then raises his eyes and responds in a calm tone: <S>That is exactly why I want you to think the attack is obvious.<E> The video then ends. The entire sequence is shot in a fixed close-up, with no cuts, zooms, or panning. The outdoor environment is quiet and restrained, with no noticeable ambient noise. Both voices are low and clear, alternating in delivery with slight spatial reflections, accurate left-right spatial positioning, and a tense, cautious tone.
general/002.mp4: The visuals present a realistic sports film style, with an overall color palette dominated by the warm gray of mountain roads, verdant green hillsides, and bright sunlight. Open natural lighting creates a tense, enduring, and speed-filled road race atmosphere. The camera employs a high-speed side-tracking shot, then gradually transitions to a front-of-the-pack lead rider's perspective, with the visual focus tightly concentrated on a cyclist sprinting at full power. The rider wears a skin-tight cycling jersey, body hunched low over the handlebars, eyes locked dead ahead, expression resolute, sweat sliding down from his forehead. His leg muscles are taut, pedaling rapidly, the wheels spinning at high speed creating slight motion blur, the sound of chain engagement crisp and clear. In the background, winding mountain roads, roadside guardrails, spectator flags, and a distant valley are visible, while other riders are pulled back by the lens into blurred figures in the background. The video begins with the rider maintaining tight formation on the mountain road, then he suddenly accelerates and breaks away from the pack. The camera follows from the side, showcasing details of the wheels, chain, and cycling jersey pulled taut by wind, as well as his all-out dynamic effort. The overall audio design features the high-speed spinning of the rider's wheels, chain engagement sounds, and his rapid yet rhythmic breathing as primary elements, with background accompaniment of wind, roadside spectator cheers, escort vehicle sounds, and distant valley echoes. The mix is layered, brisk, and tense, creating the competitive tension of a road race's final sprint.
general/003.mp4: This is a video filled with a sense of outdoor adventure and travel documentation. The video ends with her continuing to walk forward, the mountain path and distant natural scenery still visible in frame, the overall image full of the genuine vitality of a travel vlog. The camera uses a handheld front-facing selfie perspective, primarily in medium close-up selfie composition, held by the subject herself, with natural slight wobbling and pace rhythm changes during the hike—no cuts, no pans—emphasizing the authentic outdoor documentation feel of filming while walking. The overall audio design features clear, close-field female voice as the primary element, sounding natural, bright, and slightly breathless. voice/001.mp4: This realistic sci-fi film-style video captures a single-person speaking scene imbued with gentle sadness, acceptance, and life reflection.
